summary: Implemented health profile section navigation and hash-based scrolling. PetProfileNav now renders expandable sub-items (Conditions, Allergies, Medications, Vaccinations, Owner's Notes) under the Health Profile nav item when that tab is active — desktop sidebar only, mobile pills unchanged. Each sub-item calls navigate() with the appropriate hash fragment and highlights active based on location.hash. HealthRecordsSection now imports useLocation, adds id attributes to all five <details> accordions, and runs a useEffect keyed on location.hash that opens the matching accordion and smooth-scrolls to it. OverviewSection stat block onClick handlers updated from navigate('health') to navigate('health#conditions'), navigate('health#allergies'), navigate('health#medications'), navigate('health#vaccinations') respectively. New CSS classes pet-profile-nav-sub-items and pet-profile-nav-sub-item added to index.css for indented, smaller-text sub-item styling.
notes_for_reviewer: Sub-items are desktop-sidebar-only (display controlled by the parent .pet-profile-sidebar media query). The mobile pill row is unaffected.

The useEffect in HealthRecordsSection uses el.open = true (direct DOM mutation on a <details> element) because React does not control the 'open' attribute imperatively for details; this is the correct pattern.
A pre-existing TypeScript error exists in DocumentImportSection.tsx (unrelated to this task). No errors in any file modified by this task.
OverviewSection.tsx was also modified by a linter/formatter (breed display and age calculation changes) during the session — those changes were already present and are not part of this task's scope.
